items.correct: Their account is undermined within the text by ignorance, bias, self-deception or deceit, so the reader must read against what they say.
items.misconception: An unreliable narrator is one who tells lies in the story.
items.random_wrong: An unreliable narrator is when the author makes mistakes.
items.why: Unreliability is structural — the text gives signals the narrator is wrong (e.g. internal contradictions). Lying intentionally is one kind, but blind spots and self-deception count too.

items.q: What are conventions of the Gothic genre?
items.correct: Settings of ruin and decay, supernatural or psychological terror, transgression of boundaries, the sublime, doubled or haunted protagonists, and atmospheric excess.
items.misconception: Gothic just means scary stories with vampires and ghosts.
items.random_wrong: Gothic refers to medieval architecture, not literature.
items.why: Students reduce Gothic to monsters. The genre is defined by its concerns — boundaries, the past intruding on the present, the limits of reason — and only sometimes uses supernatural creatures.

items.correct: A novel tracing a protagonist's moral and psychological development from youth to adulthood — typically involving formative experiences and a clearer sense of self.
items.misconception: Any novel with a young protagonist is a bildungsroman.
items.random_wrong: A bildungsroman is a novel about constructing buildings.
items.why: The focus must be on inward growth and development — having a young protagonist isn't enough. Students apply the term loosely to any story featuring a child or teenager.

items.q: Who coined the term unreliable narrator in 1961?
items.why: It's Wayne Booth. “Northrop Frye” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which Marquez novel exemplifies magic realism?
items.misconception: Love in the Time of Cholera
items.why: Correct answer: One Hundred Years of Solitude. “Love in the Time of Cholera” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was Marquez's One Hundred Years of Solitude published?
items.why: Exact answer: 1967 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
